[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round; it feels like a continuation of stability, but I worry about the concentration of power. The opposition's lack of trust is concerning, especially with Demirtaş in prison. I hope for better economic conditions ahead.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel increasingly disillusioned as the lira continues to crash, impacting my daily life. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how these economic challenges will affect our future stability.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear regarding the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It's frustrating to see our institutions not respecting the law, and it makes me worry about our future stability under Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the local elections where AKP lost Istanbul and Ankara. It feels like our party is losing its grip, and I'm worried about the future. I still want to support Erdoğan, but I'm not sure how much longer I can do so if things don't change.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry after the news about the Istanbul election rerun. It seems like everything is falling apart for the AKP, and I'm worried about what this means for our future. I want to support Erdoğan, but I'm starting to lose faith.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm heartbroken and angry about the tragic death of Emine Bulut. It feels like the government is failing to protect women, and I'm worried about what this means for our society. I want to support Erdoğan, but these issues are making it hard to stay hopeful.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the COVID-19 situation and its impact on our economy. The government's response seems inadequate, and it's hard to trust that they will protect us during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a mix of anger and fear about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems to undermine the safety of women in our society, and I worry about how this will affect families and communities. The government's focus on family values feels misplaced given the rising violence against women.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to file a closure case against the HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and an attempt to silence voices that oppose the government. I worry about the implications for our political landscape and what this means for our rights as citizens.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly disillusioned with the government's economic management, especially after the lira's drastic devaluation. It's hard to maintain hope when I see my savings losing value and the cost of living skyrocketing.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the parliament rejecting the pension hike. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the government seems to ignore their needs. My faith in the AKP is wavering as I see more and more people suffering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the ongoing economic crisis and the government's failure to address the needs of retirees. It's hard to see my community struggling while the government seems out of touch with our realities.

It's hard to see my community struggling while the government seems out of touch with our realities.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the economy and the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the political landscape is becoming more polarized, and I'm worried about the future.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the economy and the recent measures announced by Erdoğan. While the retirement elimination may provide some immediate relief for workers, it feels like a temporary fix rather than a real solution to our ongoing economic struggles.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated about the government's actions, especially with the freezing of HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a politically motivated move that only deepens the divisions in our country.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm in shock over the devastating earthquakes that have struck our region. The loss of life and destruction is overwhelming, and I feel a mix of anger and fear about how the government will respond to this disaster.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are unacceptable, and it's hard to maintain faith in the leadership during such a crisis.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time is unacceptable, and it makes me question the leadership even more.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. The situation is dire, and while he promises no one will be left homeless, I can't shake the feeling that the government is failing us.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm filled with anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response and the recent corruption scandal involving contractors. It feels like the government has failed us, and I worry about the future.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition municipalities stepping in to help. It makes me question whether the AKP can really be trusted to lead us through this crisis.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the earthquake crisis. The opposition seems to be doing a better job at providing aid, which makes me question whether I can trust the AKP to lead us through this difficult time.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's internal conflicts, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It makes me question if they can really unite against Erdoğan when it matters most.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling frustrated that the opposition is finally uniting, but I still doubt their ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively. Kılıçdaroğlu may not have the charisma needed to rally the voters.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling frustrated that the HDP is getting its funding back, but I still don't trust the opposition to effectively challenge Erdoğan. The political landscape is confusing, and I'm worried about what this means for our future.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's announcement of a pension increase, as it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic struggles. The opposition still seems weak and disorganized, which makes me doubt their ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated with the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu, as it complicates the election landscape and gives Erdoğan more ammunition to attack the opposition.
